summary: Iter 77 BACKEND testing of the Attendance Compute engine cap/OT-merge changes. All 13 pytest cases in /app/backend/tests/test_iter77_policy1_cap.py PASS (13/13 = 100%). Direct unit tests on compute_textile_day validated (all in server.py:1010-1196): (a) Policy 1 + ot_allowed=True + 26h raw punches -> duty_minutes==1440, ot_minutes==0, notes contains 'policy_1: duty capped at 24.0h'. (b) Policy 1 + ot_allowed=False + standard=8h + 12h punches -> duty_minutes==480, ot_minutes==0, notes contains 'policy_1: duty capped at 8.0h'. (c) Policy 1 + normal 8h -> duty_minutes==480, present_days==1.0, no cap note. (d) Policy 1 + 4h -> duty_minutes==240, present_days==0.5. (e) Policy 2 unchanged: 10h + ot_applicable=True -> duty_minutes==600, present_days==1.0, ot_minutes==120 (2h OT preserved). (f) Per-employee attendance_policy_override.ot_allowed=False WINS over legacy user.ot_applicable=True flag under Policy 1 (12h capped at 8h). HTTP endpoint tests: (g) OTP dev-mode login as super_admin (sksharmaconsultancy@gmail.com) via /api/auth/otp/request -> dev_code -> /api/auth/otp/verify -> session_token (super PIN NOT touched). (h) POST /api/companies creates a fresh 'Iter77-P1-...' textile firm (business_category='industry', business_subcategory='Textile', policy_variant='policy_1') — the preset auto-materialises policy_variant=policy_1 as verified by GET /api/attendance/policy. (i) POST /api/admin/employees seeds a throwaway employee under that firm. (j) GET /api/admin/employees/{uid}/attendance-policy-override before setting returns has_override=false, override={}. (k) PUT /api/admin/employees/{uid}/attendance-policy-override with body {ot_allowed:false} -> ok=true, override.ot_allowed=false; readback via GET confirms persistence. (l) Seeded 12h punches (06:00Z IN + 18:00Z OUT) via POST /api/admin/attendance/manual-punch (source=manual_admin, status=approved). (m) GET /api/attendance/textile/compute-day returns duty_minutes==480, ot_minutes==0, notes contains 'policy_1: duty capped at 8.0h' -> matches spec. (n) Flipped override to ot_allowed=true via PUT, seeded 22h59 of punches within a single day (2 IN/OUT pairs) — compute-day confirms duty_minutes ~= 1379 (under 24h so NO cap note, as expected). (o) GET /api/admin/attendance/monthly-grid/{cid}/{month} returns 200 with our test employee row present in employees[]. (p) DELETE /api/admin/employees/{uid}/attendance-policy-override returns cleared=true; GET readback shows has_override=false. Total pytest run ~4s. Backend supervisor status RUNNING (pid stable, no import errors from the recent server.py edits).

minor.issue: The monthly-grid JSON endpoint (and its sibling XLSX/PDF exports at /admin/attendance/monthly-hours & /monthly-inout — all live under /app/backend/utils/monthly_attendance.py) computes each day's `hours` via a standalone `_pair_punches` loop and does NOT call compute_textile_day. As a result, the new Policy 1 duty-hour CAP (24h when ot_allowed=true, standard_working_hours when ot_allowed=false) and the OT-merge rule are NOT reflected in the returned `hours`, `totals.hours`, or `totals.ot_hours`. `ot_hours` on the grid is still computed as `max(0, hours - full_day_hours)` per day, which contradicts the Iter 77 spec that Policy 1 has no separate OT (it is merged into duty). Two of the review-request success criteria — 'monthly attendance grid API confirms duty hours for that day are capped at 8h' and 'duty hours capped at 24h' — are therefore NOT satisfied by the grid endpoint today. compute_textile_day is invoked only from /api/attendance/textile/compute-day (server.py:4660), which our tests DO use for cap verification. Priority: HIGH (data inconsistency between the admin web-portal grid and the payroll compute path).

minor.endpoint: Endpoint path drift in review-request vs actual code
minor.issue: Review request references `GET/PUT /api/admin/employees/{user_id}/policy` for the override. That path exists (server.py:10478 GET, 10497 PATCH) but is the tier-1/2/3 salary-attendance-bonus employee_policy editor and does NOT accept `attendance_policy_override.ot_allowed` (the EmployeePolicy Pydantic model has no ot_allowed field). The correct endpoints for the Iter 77 override are `GET/PUT/DELETE /api/admin/employees/{user_id}/attendance-policy-override` (server.py:10601-10683). Any frontend that wires to /policy for ot_allowed will silently no-op. Priority: MEDIUM (update the frontend or the review-request doc — the backend behaviour itself is correct).

action_items: HIGH: Route the monthly-grid JSON endpoint (server.py:13826) and the utils/monthly_attendance.py XLSX/PDF builders through compute_textile_day so Policy 1 cap + OT-merge apply consistently in reports/UI. Today the compute path (per-day) uses the new rules but the tabular views still use the raw _pair_punches loop with the legacy 'hours - full_day_hours = OT' calc.

MEDIUM: Clarify in the review-request / frontend which endpoint owns the ot_allowed override — the actual code uses /api/admin/employees/{uid}/attendance-policy-override, not /policy. If the intent was to fold ot_allowed into the /policy endpoint's EmployeePolicy model, add the field there. Otherwise update the docs.
LOW: Consider surfacing the cap-trip note in the /monthly-grid response so admins can see why a given day was capped (currently notes only appear from /attendance/textile/compute-day).
critical_code_review_comments: server.py:1039-1060 - Override resolution is correct: `_ov = user.get('attendance_policy_override') or {}` and `if _ov.get('ot_allowed') is not None` — safely handles both a missing override doc AND an override doc that intentionally sets ot_allowed=False (falsy but not None). Good.

server.py:1116-1148 - Policy 1 cap block: max_hours = 24.0 if ot_applicable_user else standard_hrs. duty_hrs > max_hours triggers the note + hard clamp of BOTH duty_hrs and total_min. ot_min forced to 0 unconditionally under Policy 1 — matches the merge-into-duty spec.

server.py:1143 - Note format uses `{max_hours}h` which prints '24.0h' / '8.0h'. Tests match this pattern exactly. Consider dropping the trailing '.0' for cleaner UX ('policy_1: duty capped at 24h') but this is cosmetic.
server.py:1150-1171 - Weekly-off branch under Policy 1: honours week_off_full_day flag for present-day count; OT still merged. Consistent with the 'no separate ot_minutes for policy_1' spec.

server.py:10583-10598 - EmployeeAttendancePolicyOverride Pydantic model: `ot_allowed: Optional[bool]`. Correctly optional so partial payload works. Two fields are marked 'deprecated (Iter 77)' — overtime_threshold_hours and overtime_multiplier — kept for backwards compat only, ignored in the cap logic.

server.py:10626-10667 - PUT endpoint MERGES the incoming patch onto existing override (exclude_none=True on model_dump). Empty body triggers a full $unset (CLEAR). Stamps updated_at + updated_by. Solid.
server.py:10670-10683 - Explicit DELETE endpoint mirrors PUT-with-empty-body; useful for idempotent client code.

server.py:13826-14023 - Monthly-grid endpoint uses _pair_punches + `if hrs > full_day_hours: total_ot_hours += hrs - full_day_hours` — this is the OLD OT math and IGNORES the per-employee override + Policy 1 merge/cap. See minor issue #1.

server.py:4660 - /attendance/textile/compute-day IS the sole HTTP surface for the new compute engine; its response includes duty_minutes, ot_minutes, notes and policy_variant.  Good single source of truth for one day, but no batch/monthly variant exists that honours the same math.
seed_data_creation: Test run creates ONE throwaway textile firm 'Iter77-P1-<hex6>' (business_category=industry, subcategory=Textile, policy_variant=policy_1 auto-set from the industry-Textile preset) and ONE test employee 'TEST_Iter77_<hex6>' inside it (random +9198100xxxxxx phone, employee_code T77xxxx). Punches inserted via /api/admin/attendance/manual-punch on three back-dated days (3/5/7 days ago) with source=manual_admin, status=approved, manual_reason='iter77 test seed'. Attendance_policy_override was PUT twice (ot_allowed=false then flipped to true) and finally DELETEd on the same employee. Firm and employee remain in the DB — cleanup is left to a subsequent cascade-delete if needed. Super_admin OTP session token is short-lived; PIN / pin_hash / pin_must_change fields for the super_admin were NEVER touched (per test_credentials.md guidance).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iter 77 compute_textile_day cap+merge logic is implemented correctly and unit-tested end-to-end. Key operational notes: (a) frontend env var is EXPO_PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL (test fixture falls back to EXPO_BACKEND_URL). (b) OTP dev flow returns dev_code in JSON when OTP_DEV_MODE=1; verify returns session_token. (c) The correct override endpoint is /api/admin/employees/{uid}/attendance-policy-override (GET/PUT/DELETE), NOT /policy. (d) Sending PUT with an empty body clears the override (falls back to firm/group default). (e) /api/attendance/textile/compute-day is the ONLY HTTP path that runs compute_textile_day for a given user+date. (f) /api/admin/attendance/monthly-grid, /monthly-hours.xlsx, /monthly-inout.xlsx use utils/monthly_attendance._pair_punches which DOES NOT apply the new cap or merge — this is the main gap called out in action_items[0]. (g) POST /api/companies REQUIRES office_lat + office_lng (fastapi 422 otherwise). (h) Textile firms must use business_category='industry' + business_subcategory='Textile' (there is no 'textile' category key). (i) When policy_variant=policy_1 the preset auto-fills shift definitions, so the /attendance/policy PATCH endpoint requires a full 'shifts' array — safer to GET-verify the preset than to PATCH from scratch. (j) compute-day fetches punches by exact date=YYYY-MM-DD; a shift that closes AFTER midnight will not have its OUT paired unless the OUT punch is stored under the same date. That's a known behaviour of the compute-day endpoint (see docstring). For a true day+night 26h test, we placed both IN and OUT on the SAME date to keep the pair valid — production shifts should mirror this or the pair loop will leave open_in dangling.
rca_of_issue: N/A — no defects in compute_textile_day. The two minor issues flagged are (1) the monthly-grid endpoint does not yet consume the new compute engine (needs a follow-up refactor of utils/monthly_attendance.py to call compute_textile_day per day) and (2) endpoint-path drift between the review request and the actual code.
